figures.caption: Fig. 3. Representative samples from the two poisoned fine-tuning datasets.
Fig. 1. System Architecture: ROS 2-based LLM-to-control integration pipeline translating natural language inputs into structured JSON commands for robotic actuation.

Fig. 2. Threat Model: Phase 1 illustrates supply-chain compromise via data poisoning and LoRA fine-tuning. Phase 2 depicts runtime exploitation, where the backdoored adapter generates malicious structured JSON commands that propagate through the ROS 2 control pipeline to induce unintended physical actions.
Fig. 5. ASR and CPA across evaluated LLMs under structured-output poisoning in the undefended configuration.
